review_title: OneDrive has been a huge time saver!
description: I utilize OneDrive for both company and personal use. I have found that since starting OneDrive there has been no headache of accessing a file or document when traveling or out of the office. I can easily pull anything up through the mobile application on my phone or iPad. My company is construction-based and OneDrive has really came in handy for the estimators to create folders and store documents while traveling between different job sites. Papers are no longer getting lost in the shuffle.
author: Nicole S.
pros: OneDrive is amazing for accessing documents across multiple devices. I also use it to quickly transfer items from my phone or laptop to my desktop just by uploading a document to OneDrive. Once I upload it from my phone, I can immediately access it on my desktop computer! OneDrive has really came in handy for document cloud storage.
cons: Sometimes Microsoft gives me issues with signing in, you might have to try and sign in two or three times before the system recognizes your device, but other than that it has been smooth sailing!

review_title: Microsoft OneDrive for business
description: Onedrive has a smooth integration into the Microsoft 365 ecosystem. But, sometimes it has syncing issues, resulting in duplicated files.
author: SIMNDEY BASSEY O.
pros: What i like most about OneDrive is its smooth integration into the Microsoft 365 eco-friendly system.
cons: The syncing issue. I don't like when some of my files fail to synchronize, leading to duplicated files most times.

review_title: Great functionality, lacking some management visibility and features
description: It provides great functionality for end users and alleviates some requirement from IT professionals to roll out file sharing resources and platforms. It leaves a lot to be desired in relation to it's 'cousin' - i.e on premise active directory file and user management. It overlaps a lot with Sharepoint functionality and having the sharepoint app renamed some time ago to Onedrive for Business causes a lot of confusion for both end users and junior adminsitrators
author: connor Q.
pros: Ease of sharing documents internally, and externally depending on Org settings and policies. Having native integrations and tie-ins to local file explorer allow ease of use
cons: From an administrative perspective, it leaves a lot to be desired - especially after working with on premise solutions such as Active Directory, which provides clear and intuitive management of organisational objects and permissions

review_title: Not Impressed
description: I am not impressed and hate that all my microsoft equipment hourly begs or tries to trick me into buying more backup. If you do get tricked into backing up your files you are immediately prompted that you are out of space and need to buy a subscription.
author: Kevin H.
pros: Cloud drive access across multiple devices is key in this industry. Having secure files that can be accessed at a moments notice is priceless.
cons: Accessing files with low signal is pointless because it takes too much time if it even happens at all. Issues with uploads and downloads and availability off line made it pointelss.

review_title: Plety of space, but you'll lose your freedom.
description: Overall, I like it. There are a few rough edges, things that can be polished, but it's a solid and mature product. If you don't care about Microsoft's directive to "put your files where I say", it's a good product. But if you prefer to have the freedom to choose, then you'll have to look elsewhere.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Plenty of space, more than your average cloud-drive provider, at a fraction of the cost, with "goodies" like Office. Integration with Windows. Ransomware protection. Versioning.
cons: They started limiting the folders you can sync more and more. When OneDrive was born, you were able to sync any folder you liked. Now, you can only sync your user folders. Also, inclusion/exclusion could be better.

review_title: My experience with Onedrive
description: One drive has been the best storage software i have used so far. I am able to store pretty much everything from word to PowerPoint and i love how it is secured. It is very easy to use. I used it to store both work and personal files that i do not wish to misplace. I can ipload files i wish to share with other teammates for presentations. It also have a search tab that allows me to retrieved my information very fast and efficiently.
author: Deon G.
pros: I like that i have access to my information anywhere and anytime. I can access from both my computers and cellphone.
cons: That i have to pay for storage and i that it is not able to identity identical uploads and merge them.

review_title: OneDrive - Ease to use and and organize
description: Overall, I love using one drive. I have been using it since it launched and the improvements keep coming al the time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, cost effective and Like AI help in organizing my one dive files and Media files. I love the sync of device and one drive.
cons: I find the search functionality is not great. When searching media files it has a hard time find my media and then I have to scroll to file folder then search.

review_title: onvenient Cloud Storage That Works Well Across Microsoft Tools
description: My experience with OneDrive has been positive. It is a practical and reliable cloud storage solution, especially for individuals and businesses already using the Microsoft ecosystem. The convenience of file backup, syncing, and sharing makes it a strong everyday tool. While there can be occasional syncing or usability frustrations, the overall value and accessibility make it a solid option.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: OneDrive integrates well with Microsoft 365. It makes it easy to store files, access them across devices, and share them with others when needed. Automatic syncing is very helpful, and having files backed up in the cloud adds peace of mind. It is especially useful for people who already work in Word, Excel, Outlook, and other Microsoft tools on a regular basis.
cons: Syncing issues can sometimes be frustrating, especially when files do not update properly across devices or when duplicate versions appear. Folder organization and sharing permissions can also feel a little confusing at times, particularly for less technical users. Depending on your internet connection, accessing larger files can occasionally feel slower than working locally.

review_title: One Drive enabling one copy.
description: Have been using it since its inception and am also a heavy user of its big sibling, SharePoint. Have been using these products for what feels like most of this century.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: OneDrive has great multi-platform, multi-client access. You can access from most devices with or without specialized client applications. It allows you to have one copy of a file and always know you have the latest and greatest.
cons: All sharing permissions are item by item or folder by folder, it is hard to track and manage if you get too many. Kind of like the old soup-can phone works great for 2 or maybe 3, but after that, you need more.

review_title: Solid Option at a reasonable cost.
description: Overall good value for the price and what you get. I run across 5+ devices so run into hicups sometimes but it works for the most part.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Generous storage for price, good mobile features including auto sync with Samsung Galaxy photos. Also nice that you can run multiple instances (i.e. personal and work).
cons: Can sometimes be finicky with auth and sync and when it is it can be a huge pain to resolve. I have had instances where auth keeps erroring out and have to go through reinstalls etc. to get it working.

review_title: Powerful Data Storage Tool
description: Really powerful business tool which we incorporated into our day-to-day operation to remove reliance on On-Prem server hardware for data storage, really easy to dictate which files / storage buckets are within scope (e.g. we do not upload / backup Downloads folder)
author: Jamie H.
pros: Stored files are really easy to access both using the online portal and via desktop using the OneDrive for Desktop app. It is extremely competitively priced within our Microsoft licensing model. Uploading data in bulk is really easy using the online portal meaning files can be stored quickly and efficiently. Managing stored documents is really easy given the built-in search function with the ability to change search scope from specific folders to the whole tenant (whilst obeying the users access permissions / restrictions)
cons: Fairly niche, but if two users are access the same document at once for editing, and both save, you end up with two versions of the same document which then need to be manually merged. Whilst this is preferable to one person losing data, the duplicate file has the machine name suffixed to the end (i.e. Document-MyMachine.docx) which could be a data privacy concern.

review_title: I really can’t see why people choose Microsoft over google
description: I really dislike the interface of Microsoft in general. it’s not intuitive. One drive works but it’s overall feel and layout doesn’t make natural sense
author: Breanna G.
pros: It seems to sync properly. It’s kind of easy to use. There’s a free version. It’s easy to search for files. It’s a good amount of storage. There’s a free version.
cons: I dislike the interface. It’s not intuitive. Logins in with authenticator can go in never ending loops. It’s not great for file hosting or uploading or sharing because the permissions are difficult to set. Drag and drop not as easy as Google Drive. The online versions of excel and word always mess up the document. Feels like it’s trying to force me into using the paid one.

review_title: Microsoft OneDrive 2025
description: I use OneDrive everyday it allows me to keep multiple documents and rarely have issues when opening the documents the issues are related to the internet connection more than the product. Excellent product
author: Theresa J.
pros: I prefer OneDrive to Google Drive because it’s easier to access the documents the way they are created without any formatting issues.
cons: When I try to download certain documents from my email on the computer or phone I occasionally have issues saving properly I run into errors that force me ruse Google Drive

review_title: Great tool, unsure of its full potential
description: It has been a useful tool for me, and store almost all of my work in One Drive. Although I use it, I don't think a lot of my team members utilize it to its full extent.
author: David F.
pros: I love that it brings files and workspace into one area where everyone can collaborate and update documents together. I really like the autosave function.
cons: After granting access to a folder for other team members, it is unclear to me if they only have access to those files within the folder, or if they can then back track to other files I don't want to share with them.

review_title: Honest Review of OneDrive
description: I'm very pleased with OneDrive. I feel that it's a service that is worth the money. No system is perfect, but it delivers what it promises.
author: Aaron S.
pros: I really like that I feel confident that my files are private and protected, that make me want to continue using their service in the future.
cons: Sometimes while using my Mac the OneDrive desktop features get disconnected and I have to reconnect them manually.

review_title: We no longer have a need for on premise servers!
description: Overall, it's great to be able to sign in from any device and have access to your files like you are sitting in your office.
author: Travis B.
pros: I like that OneDrive/SharePoint are included in a Microsoft Business Premium License. We have gotten rid of all physical servers and moved everything to OneDrive.
cons: It's not as fast as an on premise server. Having to re-login all of the time can be a bit frustrating.

review_title: Great storage solution
description: Very good. I now have three ms 365 sharepoinr folder systems synched to my mobile for charitiws plus my own personal onedrive
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Reliable, auto backup, synch to pc and mobile, easy to configure with folders etc giving immediate a cess in office and on the go
cons: Quite tricky to synch 3 different ms366 accounts to pc for charities i work with - but ms support heloed me do it

review_title: OneDrive- Easy and efficient.
description: OneDrive is a tool we use everyday to store a large amount of documents used across the business. It is an efficient way of doing this and it's easy access and search features enable us to retrieve files quickly.
author: Sam K.
pros: We use this everyday as a virtual tool for different people in the business to access shared files and as well as different teams to upload any files that are relevant to theem so they can all be downloaded from OneDrive as opposed to be saved on each desktop. This also means documents being used are the most current as only the updated are stored here and no-on is using anything that is out of date. It is simple to use and we have different folders for teams and for shared documents. It is easy to search within one folder or just the entire One Drive itself. We have a lot saved onto there and the question of storage had never arisen, neither has the need to contact customer service.
cons: The main issues we have with OneDrive are internal. I guess on occasion someone has accidentally saved a change (as when you close the document it sdaves) but that is why we keep back ups.

review_title: OneDrive provides convenient built-in backup, with some caveats to be aware of
description: Overall good product, but more fine-tuning is needed, with easier configuration controls and a more reliable communication of the status of the product (offline vs. online, i.e., backup fully working and complete vs. halted due to loss of authentication).
author: Joel S.
pros: Integration into operating system (Windows) is handy, with OneDrive working relatively seamlessly in the background once setup.
cons: It is just unreliable enough to where I don't fully trust it, as occasionally you'll be logged out of OneDrive, with it no longer backing files up, and you won't even realize it.

review_title: Reliable Cloud Storage with Strong Microsoft Integration
description: My overall experience with OneDrive has been positive, especially within the Microsoft ecosystem. It provides reliable cloud storage with good syncing across devices, making it easy to access files anywhere. The platform integrates seamlessly with Office apps like Word, Excel, and Teams, which improves productivity. While occasional sync delays can happen, it remains a dependable solution for both personal and professional use.
author: Stefan D.
pros: OneDrive’s biggest strength is its deep integration with Microsoft 365, making file sharing and collaboration very efficient. The interface is clean and easy to navigate, even for non-technical users. File uploads are fast, and document management features like version history and real-time editing are very useful. The search functionality works well for locating files quickly, and flexible storage plans provide good scalability.
cons: OneDrive can sometimes experience sync issues, especially when handling large files or unstable connections. Conflict copies and delays in updating files across devices can be frustrating. The search feature, while decent, occasionally struggles with very large datasets. Customer support is not always responsive, particularly for non-enterprise users. Additionally, the interface can feel limited when managing complex folder structures.

review_title: The Highest Height of File Applications
description: My experience has overall been very pleasant. Onedrive is the one shared file application that I have used and I could not imagine using another form of app.
author: Jhovanny A.
pros: I am not the most tech savy when it comes to files. Thus, having a easy, simple, organized and efficient network is very important to me. It excels in network sharing (especially when we consider the amount of space that is available to you). Whether, its scanning, uploading, transferring, editing or examination: all my files are in good hands.
cons: My least favorite features is the shared docs interface. I wish there were options to delete from our history or drive dos that were shared with us. I also wish there were ways to clean up the interface.

review_title: OneDrive is Great for Business!
description: My overall experience with OneDrive has been amazing! I truly only have one complaint, which is the system crashing but it happens very rarely. Also, I am never working on a document when it does this, it usually occurs when I first open the software. The software is usually very speedy and I thoroughly enjoy using OneDrive for work.
author: Barrett D.
pros: The main aspect I enjoy about OneDrive is the ability to share documents with people; they are also allowed to edit the document which updates on my document also. To save files on OneDrive is very simple and I love that you can save a document on one computer and go to another computer and your document will be there. The system is very easy to navigate and never caused me any issues. I haven't had to call for customer support due to the system being easy to understand.
cons: My only complaint about OneDrive is that it will crash when opening the software. It doesn't happen all the time but at least once per week. This complaint is not a deal-breaker for me because it is very easy to open the software back up. I am not sure if it is OneDrive crashing or my computer but it happens on all work computers.

review_title: OneDrive for Cloud-Based Storage
description: OneDrive has been a great resource to store documents and prevent loss of data. There is a small learning curve to using the cloud-based software, but once you learn to navigate how to save files and access, it is streamlined and carries resources from device to device. I use it everyday and can't imagine working without it.
author: Alexandra V.
pros: OneDrive is a great resource to save documents for easy access across devices and teams. We utilize the software across our company, and it has helped us share documents and resources easily, and prevent data loss when things go awry. OneDrive operates very similarly to other cloud based software, and it is a fast learning curve. I use both the extension and actual OneDrive portal through Microsoft across multiple devices with no issue.
cons: Overall, OneDrive is easy to navigate. However, there is a learning curve to finding it on the device and learning how to save to OneDrive versus just to your device. This is pretty common for cloud-based storage, but it is something in which to be cognizant.

review_title: LifeSaver!
author: Stephanie M.
pros: I use OneDrive every single day, all day long, in my profession. I use it for file management, document uploads, storage, document creation, file sharing, etc. It's extremely easy to use and integrates very easily with many other programs. I wouldn't be as efficient as I am without it.
cons: I have not found a single thing I don't like about OneDrive. Everything I need is right at my fingertips.

review_title: Smooth and Reliable File Management with Microsoft OneDrive at E-Service Park
description: My overall experience with OneDrive has been very positive. At E-Service Park, we use it to manage and share important documents, and it has made our daily operations much smoother. The platform is easy to use, secure, and accessible from anywhere, which helps in coordinating tasks efficiently. Features like automatic sync and integration with tools like Microsoft Excel make it even more useful. Overall, it has improved our productivity and simplified file management without any major issues.
author: Hari K.
pros: OneDrive is easy to use, secure, and lets me access and share files anytime, improving daily productivity.
cons: At times, syncing can be slow or get stuck, especially with large files or poor internet. Also, managing shared folders and permissions can feel a bit confusing initially.
